ranked_movies.title: The Bourne Identity
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended across multiple comments for its engaging plot and action sequences. Considered one of the best in the thriller genre, with specific praise for its European setting and realistic action.

ranked_movies.title: Se7en
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a top-tier thriller, especially in the context of serial killer movies. It's praised for its dark tone and twist ending, making it a staple in the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Silence of the Lambs
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently brought up as a benchmark for psychological thrillers. Its inclusion in multiple discussions underscores its enduring popularity and critical acclaim.

ranked_movies.title: Zodiac
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for its detailed investigation process and pacing. Often compared to Se7en for its procedural elements and realism.

ranked_movies.title: The Hand That Rocks the Cradle
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the context of classic thrillers, and noted for its impact in the 90s thriller era. It's a representative of its time but still holds up.

ranked_movies.title: Basic Instinct
ranked_movies.reason: Another frequently cited film from the 90s, noted for its erotic thriller elements and strong performances, especially by Sharon Stone.

ranked_movies.title: Single White Female
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up multiple times as a solid example of a thriller from the 90s that still resonates. Its title alone makes it memorable.

ranked_movies.title: Vettaiyadu Vilaiyadu
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its action and as a benchmark in Tamil cinema. It's highlighted as a film that set standards for later action thrillers.

ranked_movies.title: The Skeleton Key
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as an underrated horror/thriller with a memorable twist. Its mention in multiple contexts shows its staying power.

ranked_movies.title: The Descent
ranked_movies.reason: Often brought up in horror and thriller discussions for its claustrophobic tension and effective scares.

ranked_movies.title: Session 9
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a deeply unsettling psychological thriller. Its reputation for being disturbing keeps it in conversations.

ranked_movies.title: The Ritual
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its folk horror elements and pacing. It's a more recent film that gets recommended often for those looking for something new but effective.

ranked_movies.title: The Cell
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up for its visual style and unique premise. It's a film that divides audiences but is often remembered for its ambition.

ranked_movies.title: The Guest
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a fun, action-oriented thriller with a strong lead performance. It's seen as a good example of modern genre blending.

ranked_movies.title: Oculus
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that doesn't get enough attention but is well-regarded by those who've seen it, especially for its clever use of horror tropes.

ranked_movies.title: The Vanishing
ranked_movies.reason: The original (1988) is often cited as one of the most unsettling thrillers ever made. Its ending is particularly noted.

ranked_movies.title: The Others
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up in mystery/thriller contexts for its twist and atmosphere. It's a film that's grown in reputation over the years.

ranked_movies.title: The Hand That Rocks the Cradle
ranked_movies.reason: While not as widely discussed as others, it's included here as it was specifically mentioned in the context of thrillers from the 90s and represents an era.

ranked_movies.title: The Cell
ranked_movies.reason: Included for its visual style and psychological elements. It's a film that's often brought up in design discussions.

ranked_movies.title: The Ninth Gate
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its atmosphere and mystery. It's a film that has a dedicated following and is often recommended to those who like slower-burn thrillers.
